Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Respondent's Motion for Attorney's Fees is DENIED. The parties agreed in their contract that arbitration-related fees will be paid by each respective party. Lease Section 3.1.(c)(iv). Parties further agreed that arbitration within the meaning of the contract does not only include the proceeding before an arbitrator, but also involves related court proceedings. See section 3.1 (c) "if the fair market rent is determined by arbitration, the award rendered in any arbitration hereunder may be entered in any court having jurisdiction and shall be final and binding upon the parties." Discussed. Parties submitt the tenative ruling. Petitioner requests changes to the Courts proposed schedule. The COurt orders parties to meet and confer.
Parties report that they have have agreed on a new schedule. The Court rules as follows: Continued to May 9, 2018. By no later than April 19, 2018 by 4:00 p.m. each party shall file a list of 5 proposed arbitrators accompanied by CVs, and a brief not to exceed 3 pages addressing why each proposed arbitrator is qualified. Each proposed arbitrator shall be ready and willing to conduct the arbitration. By no later than April 30, 2018 parties are to meet and confer regarding each of the 10 proposed arbitrators. By no later than May 4, 2018 parties shall file a joint brief specifying objections to each proposed arbitrator, if any, and response to the objection. The joint brief shall be in a table format: (1) Colum One: Name of Proposed Arbitrator; (2) Column Two: Objection; (3) Colum Three: Response to Objection. In the alternative, by no later than May 4, 2018 parties may submit a joint statement of name(s) of the agreed upon arbitrator(s) from the lists provided on April 19, 2018 or any other proposed arbitrator (accompanied by CVs and brief regarding qualifications, not to exceed 1 page).
